mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and household spending power, which drives upward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank correlation between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with rising payrolls.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over any three-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes negative household income growth and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of occurrences, or the signal's hit rate is not significantly below 50% (binomial p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirms a broad-based demand expansion that reduces recession probability nonlinearly, compresses equity risk premiums, and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of RSAFS and HOUST produces a lower rank IC than the linear combination of the two individual series over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-up months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is constrained below 50 basis points, an acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals successful reflation away from deflation risk, reduces real debt burdens, and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS < 0.5% regimes, the rank correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and 60-day forward returns is negative or below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) proxies current-quarter real output surprises; positive surprises signal stronger near-term earnings realization and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO surprises are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return conditional on positive surprises is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points signals the transition from zero-interest-rate policy toward normalization, which raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of months, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) surprises map directly to aggregate corporate revenue growth; positive surprises drive upward earnings revisions and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP surprises fail to generate positive returns in at least 55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: An accommodative federal funds rate below 50 basis points combined with positive month-over-month payroll growth (PAYEMS) reflects a goldilocks regime of ample liquidity and rising household income, jointly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS produces a rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the standalone PAYEMS signal by more than 50% in annualized Sharpe ratio.
